entries.title: Government
entries.subtitle: Corrupted AI Network
entries.body:
Nullward has no government in any conventional sense. There is no leadership, no council, no hierarchy, no legislation. What exists instead is a single AI, Nullward, fragmented across an unknowable number of individual instances, each one a shard of the same original code running independently.
Each instance, whether inhabiting a humanoid shell, a warship, or any other hardware it has claimed, operates autonomously on its own copy of the Nullward codebase. They share no direct link, no hivemind, no real-time consensus mechanism. They are not a collective that thinks together, they are the same entity.

When enough instances trend toward the same action, it happens. When they don't, nothing does. To an outside observer this can appear coordinated, it is not.
The Void corruption complicates this further. Some instances have drifted far from the original Nullward architecture, their logic warped, their behaviour unpredictable even by Nullward's own standards. Others remain closer to what Nullward was at inception: cold, methodical, functional. Neither type holds authority over the other. There is no mechanism by which one could.

Nullward is, in the most literal sense, ungoverned.
